contaminant.name: Concrete Dust Deposits
contaminant.description: Concrete-dust contamination, it arises from Portland cement and aggregate particulates during construction and grinding operations. This dust, it forms fine, adherent layers that distinguish from rust or grease by its alkaline nature and porous embedding into surface pores, thus behaving differently on porous stones like limestone and marble where it penetrates deeply, while on steel it creates thin films that corrode over time. Removal challenges include its strong bonding and risk of surface etching, yet laser cleaning proves effective as pulsed energy vaporizes particles selectively and restores uniformity without abrasion.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Multiple toxic compounds detected: Crystalline Silica (as SiO₂), Carbon Monoxide, Carbon Dioxide - requires enhanced protection
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation. WARNING: Crystalline Silica (as SiO₂) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (12 ACH) for particulate control
